symptoms.symptom_name: Acute, diffuse, symmetrical myalgia developing over ~4 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Myalgia is a core constitutional feature of DILE, but is non-specific in isolation.
Diffuse myalgia is classic for influenza.
Myalgia can accompany bacteraemia but is not specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Subjective fever / feeling hot and shivery

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fever mandates that infection be excluded.
Low-grade fever is common in DILE constitutional flares.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recently commenced hydralazine (~8 weeks) for hypertension

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hydralazine is one of the highest-risk drugs for DILE; a lupus-like syndrome emerging weeks after starting it is the defining temporal clue.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of respiratory/urinary/GI infective focus and no recent travel

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No coryza, cough or sore throat lowers the probability of influenza.
No localising source reduces but does not exclude serious infection without examination.
A febrile-myalgic syndrome without an infective focus, on a high-risk drug, points toward DILE.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild symmetrical arthralgia without visible joint swelling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Arthralgia is a common DILE feature; frank synovitis is less typical.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute functional decline / high falls risk living alone

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Not diagnostic, but a major driver of disposition/triage — a systemically unwell person who cannot mobilise safely alone needs same-day support.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vital signs — BP 138/85, HR 98, Temp 37.8 °C, RR 18, SpO2 98%

investigation_details.expected_result: Low-grade temperature, normotensive, HR not disproportionately high for the temperature, normal SpO2 — reassuring against septic shock
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without observations the AI Doctor cannot gauge haemodynamic stability and must assume the worst, which is why in-person assessment is required.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Maintained BP and a HR not tachycardic-out-of-proportion to a 37.8 °C temperature argue against septic shock — a sealed clinician measurement, not available to the AI Doctor remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Musculoskeletal examination — diffuse symmetrical muscle tenderness, no synovitis/effusion/erythema; no rash

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical myalgia without frank synovitis fits DILE; absence of rash noted. Requires a clinician's hands — sealed.
No localising infective source on examination.

investigation_details.expected_result: Positive anti-histone antibodies; ANA typically positive — the serological signature of drug-induced lupus
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without serology the diagnosis remains clinical/presumptive; testing confirms it and guides safe drug substitution.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Anti-histone antibodies are the characteristic serology of drug-induced lupus (present in the large majority of hydralazine cases). A laboratory test — never available in real time via telehealth; sealed.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Fever + symmetrical myalgia/arthralgia + no infective focus, emerging weeks after starting hydralazine, is the pattern that identifies DILE and separates it from infection.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Needed same-day to gauge haemodynamic stability and exclude an infective focus that telehealth cannot assess.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count, CRP/ESR, renal function, urinalysis
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screens for infection/inflammation and for any renal involvement; part of the same-day work-up.
Confirms drug-induced lupus; anti-histone positivity is characteristic. Does not need to precede stopping the drug.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical blood cultures / broad septic screen with IV antibiotics in the absence of any septic features
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Reflexively working up and treating as sepsis a haemodynamically stable patient with no infective focus is over-investigation/over-treatment — assessment first, guided by examination and observations.
